steps.title: Identify the demonstration
steps.content: The passage establishes that ecological information can be maintained in Indigenous songs, then shows that the Kwakwaka'wakw clam-garden practice (preserved through Wathl'thla's songs) corresponded to growth in clam size and abundance despite increased harvesting pressure. The blank must capture what this finding demonstrates.

steps.title: Match the demonstration to a choice
steps.content: The finding demonstrates that the practices preserved in Indigenous songs aren't merely cultural records — they encode genuinely effective ecological knowledge worth taking seriously. Choice B captures this: the song-preserved practices constitute valid ecological knowledge. The other choices either overclaim exclusivity (A), deny the demonstrated link between the practices and the outcome (C), or wrongly elevate one form of evidence over the other (D). That matches choice B — B is correct.
